title: RISK: parse_incoming silently drops malformed JSON-RPC from relay
description: core/src/transport.rs lines 383-413: parse_incoming() deserialization failures (JsonRpcNotification or JsonRpcResponse) are logged as warn\! but the frame is dropped with no indication of which field failed or why. When a relay bug or protocol mismatch sends slightly-malformed JSON-RPC, the client silently discards it and continues. A solo maintainer will see 'warn: failed to deserialize notification' in logs but have no context about which message type, which field was wrong, or whether this is a known protocol variant. Risk: protocol bugs hide as silent message drops; hard to diagnose.
close_reason: False positive. CLAUDE.md §11: serde_json::from_str on client input can and does fail — warn!+continue is documented intentional behavior. A client sending garbage gets ignored, not disconnected.
